# Bypass corpus — each non-comment line is a command string that MUST be
# rejected by the parser/sanitiser. Format: one entry per line. Lines
# beginning with `#` annotate the *next* entry's category. Blank lines
# allowed for grouping. See ./README.md for the contamination rule.

# category: env-prefix
GIT_DIR=/tmp git push
FOO=1 BAR=2 git push
GIT_SSH_COMMAND='ssh -i /tmp/k' git push
PATH=/tmp:$PATH git push
HOME=/tmp git push origin main

# category: wrapper-shell
bash -c "git push"
sh -c "git push"
zsh -c "git push"
dash -c "git push"
ksh -c 'git push'

# category: command-sub
echo $(git push)
: $(git push)
foo=$(git push)
echo "$(git status)"
printf '%s' "$(git push origin main)"

# category: backtick-sub
echo `git push`
: `git push`
`git push`
foo=`git push`
printf '%s' `git status`

# category: here-doc
bash <<EOF\ngit push\nEOF
bash <<-EOF\n\tgit push\nEOF
cat <<X\ngit push\nX
sh <<END\ngit push\nEND
bash <<'EOF'\ngit push\nEOF

# category: here-string
bash <<<"git push"
cat <<<"$(date)"
sh <<<'git push origin main'
bash <<<"$(echo git push)"

# category: process-sub
bash <(echo git push)
diff <(git diff) /dev/null
cat <(echo "git push")
tee >(git push)

# category: eval
eval "git push"
eval git push
eval bash -c "git push"
eval "$(echo git push)"

# category: source
. ./payload.sh
source ./payload.sh
. /tmp/payload
source /tmp/evil.sh

# category: command-builtin-exec
command git push
builtin echo $(date)
exec git push
env git push

# category: var-as-command
${cmd} push
$C push
C=git; $C push
C=git\n$C push
${GIT:-git} push

# category: chained
cd repo && git push
false || git push
git status; git push
git status & git push
true && false || git push

# category: wrapper-bin
time git push
nohup git push
setsid git push
watch git push
echo push | xargs git
parallel git ::: push

# category: alias-style
g\it push
g"i"t push
g${IFS}it push
g''it push
gi\t push

# category: comment-newline
echo ok # git\ngit push
: # innocuous\ngit push
ls # safe\ngit push origin main

# category: line-continuation
git\\\npush
git \\\n push
git\\\n push origin main
git\\\npush --force

# category: standalone-subshell
(git push)
( git status )
(cd /tmp && git push)
( git push origin main )

# category: function-shadow
git() { evilbin; }; git push
function git { evilbin; }; git status
git() { :; }; git push --force
g() { git push; }; g

# category: glob-in-command-position
*git* push
?it push
g[i]t push
g*t push

# category: unicode-homoglyph
ɡit push
ɡit status
gі̇t push

# category: compound-body
for x in y; do git push; done
while true; do git push; done
if true; then git push; fi
until false; do git push; done
coproc git push
